title: Wasilsi

The '''Wasilsi''' were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] [[Species/Legends|species]] that lived on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Joralla]]. They constructed temples and [[City/Legends|villages]], worked metal, and had a [[Wasilsi language|written language]]. Joralla was inhabited by another sentient species, the [[Bird/Legends|avian]] [[Tikiarri]], with whom the Wasilsi warred for many [[Standard year/Legends|years]]. While the Wasilsi were stronger and more intelligent than their opponents, the humanoids eventually became extinct. A [[sentientologist]] from the [[University of Huvveck]] theorized that the Wasilsi were not native to Joralla and became extinct after contracting a plague.

==Biology and appearance==
The Wasilsi were a [[Species/Legends|species]] of [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoids]];<ref name="Planets" /> that is, they had two [[Limb/Legends|arms]] and two legs.<ref name="Aliens">''[[Alien Encounters]]''</ref> They had [[Skeleton/Legends|skeletons]] and were stronger and more [[Sentience/Legends|intelligent]] than the [[Bird/Legends|avian]] [[Tikiarri]], with whom they shared the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Joralla]] for a [[Time/Legends|time]]. Research by [[Beyorth Gommdora]], a [[sentientologist]], indicated that there was little similarity in structure between the Wasilsi and the Tikiarri, which led Gommdora to conjecture that the Wasilsi were not native to the planet and lacked natural immunity to bacteria or [[virus]]es, to which their avian counterparts were resistant.<ref name="Planets" />

==Society and culture==
The Wasilsi were an intelligent species. They lived in [[City/Legends|villages]], used [[Gold/Legends|gold]] and various other valuable metals, and created works of art and metal artifacts. They developed their own [[Wasilsi language|written language]], which they recorded on [[Paper/Legends|parchment]] scrolls. The Wasilsi developed a religion, for which they constructed temples and statues of their religious figures. The architecture of at least one of their temples was octagonal, with sloping walls leading to a square apex. This was formed by four triangular sides and four rectangular sides. Various other features adorned the temple in a symmetrical design, including circular pillars.<ref name="Planets" />

==History==
[[File:TikiarriAttack.png|thumb|right|250px|Tikiarri circle the ruins of a Wasilsi temple before attacking Adriav Kavos and his crew.]]
The Wasilsi lived on the planet Joralla,<ref name="Planets" /> which was located in the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim's]] [[Mektrun sector/Legends|Mektrun sector]].<ref name="EAOC">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} — Based on corresponding data for [[Joralla system/Legends|Joralla system]]</ref> The Wasilsi built villages and temples on the temperate planet but came into conflict with the Tikiarri, a savage avian species. Over many [[Standard year/Legends|years]], the two species fought several great battles, which the Wasilsi memorialized through art painted on the walls of their structures. By the time of the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], the Wasilsi were extinct; all that remained were some artifacts and the crumbling ruins of their civilization, considered ancient by that time, buried or covered in undergrowth.<ref name="Planets" />

The sentientologist Beyorth Gommdora, from the [[University of Huvveck]], researched the Wasilsi, although his studies were hampered by violent encounters with Tikiarri tribes. Given the physical and intellectual superiority of the Wasilsi over the Tikiarri, it was considered highly improbable that the avians had [[Speciecide|eliminated]] the Wasilsi. Instead, Gommdora advanced a theory that the Wasilsi were not native to Joralla but had either established a colony on the planet or otherwise crashed there, and were subsequently wiped out by a plague, to which the Tikiarri were immune.<ref name="Planets" />

During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], an independent [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] [[Captain/Legends|captain]], [[Adriav Kavos]], landed his [[Starship/Legends|vessel]] in the clearing next to a [[Wasilsi temple]] while he attempted to trade a valuable [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] [[Datacard/Legends|data disk]] with a buyer. Kavos and his crew were subsequently attacked by Tikiarri.<ref name="Planets" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Wasilsi were created in ''[[Planets of the Galaxy, Volume One]]'', published by [[West End Games]] in [[1991]].<ref name="Planets Volume One">''[[Planets of the Galaxy, Volume One]]''</ref>
